name: Aisha
manager_notes: Aisha is in week four, remote, and has started driving planning threads, but she still looks uncertain in cross-functional meetings when priorities conflict. I need to understand what is still unclear around decision rights, partner expectations, and how to run alignment without over-indexing on consensus. She has good energy, but I do not want her spending it decoding unspoken norms. This meeting should surface the specific friction points and translate them into practical support and clearer boundaries.
answers: Settling in, but still learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
Can draft plans, but unsure when to decide versus escalate.
Cross-team conversations move fast — sometimes leaves unsure who committed to what.
Remote makes it harder to read context others pick up naturally.
Wants to drive alignment but worries about creating noise by over-communicating.
Still needs a clearer picture of how roadmap tradeoffs are finalized here.
A consistent partner check-in cadence would help validate assumptions earlier.
With explicit decision boundaries for the quarter, could lead much more confidently.
Wants to run one planning thread with explicit decision boundaries before next check-in.
